meta.name: Injectable Contraceptive / Intracranial Hypertension — Settlement Point Matrix
meta.mdl: In re: Injectable Contraceptive Products Liability Litigation (MDL)
meta.disclaimer: SYNTHETIC — FOR DEMONSTRATION ONLY. Real MDL settlement matrices are negotiated between the PSC and defendants and administered by a special master; they are confidential and case-specific. This file is a structurally faithful stand-in, authored to exercise the engine. A firm supplies their own matrix; nothing here is a valuation opinion.
meta.note: Read by lib/matrix.ts. The evaluator is deterministic: score(records, matrix) is a pure function, so the same record set and matrix version always produce the same points. The LLM extracts fields; it never scores. Every point must trace to a cited record, and a factor whose only support is unreviewed low-confidence text is withheld rather than guessed.

tiers.label: Tier 1
tiers.description: Highest-value stratum: long exposure, confirmed diagnosis, permanent deficit, no confounders.

tiers.label: Tier 2
tiers.description: Confirmed diagnosis with strong exposure and causation support; deficit unresolved or resolving.

tiers.label: Tier 3
tiers.description: Qualifying injury with partial documentation or material confounders.

tiers.label: Tier 4
tiers.description: Qualifying but weakly documented; short exposure or unconfirmed diagnosis.

tiers.label: Below matrix
tiers.description: Does not reach the minimum documented threshold for a scored award.

gates.label: Documented exposure to the product
gates.requirement: At least one documented administration or prescription of the injectable contraceptive.

gates.label: Qualifying diagnosis
gates.requirement: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension / pseudotumor cerebri (ICD-10 G93.2) affirmatively diagnosed.

gates.label: Diagnosis follows exposure
gates.requirement: First documented diagnosis post-dates the first documented administration.

factors.label: Exposure duration and intensity
factors.requirement: Number of documented administrations of the injectable contraceptive.
factors.why_it_matters: Dose-response is the spine of general causation. Administration count is the most defensible measure of it, because each dose is an independently documented event.

bands.label: 6 or more documented administrations
3-5 documented administrations
1-2 documented administrations
No administration documented

factors.label: Diagnostic confirmation
factors.requirement: Lumbar puncture opening pressure above 25 cm H2O, or papilledema on dilated fundus exam.
factors.why_it_matters: An opening pressure measurement is objective and reproducible. A clinical impression is not. This factor is where most inventory cases collapse under defense scrutiny.

bands.label: Opening pressure above 25 cm H2O documented
Papilledema documented without qualifying opening pressure
Clinical diagnosis only

factors.label: Secondary causes excluded on neuroimaging
factors.requirement: Venous sinus thrombosis and intracranial mass excluded by MRI/MRV.
factors.why_it_matters: The diagnosis is only *idiopathic* if the secondary causes were ruled out. This factor is scored from a negative finding — the absence is the evidence, and it is exactly the evidence a keyword extractor throws away.

bands.label: Secondary causes excluded on imaging
Not excluded on the available records

factors.label: Treating-clinician causation statement
factors.requirement: Treating clinicians attributing the injury to the exposure, in the record, in their own words.
factors.why_it_matters: A statement written by a treating physician in a contemporaneous chart note is worth far more than a retained expert's opinion, and two independent clinicians is the threshold at which the attribution stops looking like one doctor's theory.

bands.label: Two or more independent treating clinicians attribute causation
One treating clinician attributes causation
No treating-clinician attribution in the record

factors.label: No pre-exposure history of the condition
factors.requirement: Medical records covering at least 12 months before first exposure, showing no intracranial hypertension and no chronic headache disorder.
factors.why_it_matters: You cannot prove absence from records that do not exist. Without pre-exposure coverage the defence will argue the condition predated the drug, and there is nothing in the file to rebut it.

bands.label: Pre-exposure records confirm the condition was absent
Pre-exposure history of the condition is documented

factors.evidence_needed: Primary-care and any specialist records covering the 12 months before the first administration.

factors.label: Obesity confounder
factors.requirement: BMI at or around the time of diagnosis.
factors.why_it_matters: Obesity is the single most-cited alternative cause of intracranial hypertension and the defence's first move. A plaintiff with a normal BMI removes it — which makes this one of the most valuable normal-range vital signs in the entire chart.

bands.label: BMI below 30 — obesity confounder removed
BMI 30 or above — obesity available as an alternative cause

factors.evidence_needed: Any record documenting height/weight or BMI within 12 months of diagnosis.

factors.label: Ongoing treatment burden
factors.requirement: Still on disease-directed therapy at the most recent record.
factors.why_it_matters: Continuing therapy is the difference between a resolved episode and an ongoing injury, and it is the anchor for future-medicals.

bands.label: On disease-directed therapy at the latest record
Therapy concluded / resolved

factors.label: Permanent visual deficit
factors.requirement: Ophthalmologic examination at least 6 months after diagnosis documenting a persistent field defect or vision loss.
factors.why_it_matters: Permanence is the largest single driver of award band in every matrix of this shape. It cannot be established from an exam taken weeks after diagnosis, no matter what that exam shows.

rule.deficit_match: field defect|vision loss|visual field|enlarged blind spot|rnfl

bands.label: Persistent deficit documented at 6+ months post-diagnosis
No persistent deficit documented

factors.evidence_needed: A current neuro-ophthalmology examination (visual fields + OCT) dated at least 6 months after the diagnosis date.

factors.label: Alternative headache etiology
factors.requirement: Personal or family history of migraine or other primary headache disorder.
factors.why_it_matters: Not fatal, but it gives the defence a competing explanation for the presenting symptom and it will appear in their expert report. Better to know it is in the file before they find it.

bands.label: Migraine history documented — defence-usable confounder
No competing headache etiology in the record

factors.label: Exposure corroborated by independent sources
factors.requirement: Exposure documented by two or more independent facilities, not solely by patient report.
factors.why_it_matters: A single clinic's log is impeachable. The same exposure appearing in an unrelated facility's chart is not. This is also the factor most often satisfied by records the firm already has and never cross-referenced.

bands.label: Exposure corroborated across independent facilities
Exposure documented by a single source
